title: NodeSplittingHard
lean_code_with_comments:
def nodeSplittingHard (G : CDMG Node) (hG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ‚äÜ G.V) :
    CDMG (SplitNode Node) where
  J := G.J.image SplitNode.unsplit ‚ą™ W.image SplitNode.copy1
  V := (G.V \ W).image SplitNode.unsplit ‚ą™ W.image SplitNode.copy0
  hJV_disj := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hJV_disj G W
  E := G.E.image (fun e => (toCopy1 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2))
  hE_subset := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hE_subset G W
  L := G.L.image (fun e => (toCopy0 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2))
  hL_subset := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_subset G W
  hL_irrefl := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_irrefl G W
  hL_symm := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_symm G W
lean_code_without_comments:
def nodeSplittingHard (G : CDMG Node) (hG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ‚äÜ G.V) :
    CDMG (SplitNode Node) where
  J := G.J.image SplitNode.unsplit ‚ą™ W.image SplitNode.copy1
  V := (G.V \ W).image SplitNode.unsplit ‚ą™ W.image SplitNode.copy0
  hJV_disj := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hJV_disj G W
  E := G.E.image (fun e => (toCopy1 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2))
  hE_subset := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hE_subset G W
  L := G.L.image (fun e => (toCopy0 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2))
  hL_subset := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_subset G W
  hL_irrefl := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_irrefl G W
  hL_symm := by exact nodeSplittingHard_hL_symm G W
lean_explanation:
Each binder of `nodeSplittingHard` carries one piece of the LN's setup. `G : CDMG Node` is the input graph $G = (J, V, E, L)$, with `Node : Type*` as the ambient vertex carrier and `[DecidableEq Node]` required for `Finset.image` to be constructible. The hypothesis `hG : G.IsCADMG` realises the LN's 'Let $G$ be a CADMG': in this development there is no separate `CADMG` structure type ‚ÄĒ `def_3_7` defines `IsCADMG` as a propositional predicate `IsAcyclic` over a `CDMG`, so 'Let $G$ be a CADMG' formalises as a `CDMG` plus an acyclicity proposition. The pair `(W : Finset Node)` and `(hW : W ‚äÜ G.V)` realises 'Let $W \subseteq V$ be a subset of output nodes'.

The return type is `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`. `SplitNode Node`, reused verbatim from `def_3_11`, is the three-constructor inductive with `unsplit`, `copy0`, `copy1`. The SWIG reading fixes `.copy0 ‚ÜĒ ^o` (output side) and `.copy1 ‚ÜĒ ^i` (input side). The LN's disjointness assertions $W^o \cap V = W^i \cap V = W^o \cap J = W^i \cap J = W^o \cap W^i = \emptyset$ hold automatically by typing: the three constructors of `SplitNode` are distinct by definition.

The four LN clauses i.‚Äďiv.\ become the four named data fields. Each is built with `Finset.image`, Lean's primitive for the set-builder $\{f(x) \mid x \in s\}$; this matches the LN's mathematical phrasing and preserves the `DecidableEq`-driven decidability of finite membership needed downstream. The LN's notational shorthand $v^o := v^i := v$ for $v \notin W$ is implemented by two helpers from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`: `toCopy0 W v` returns `.copy0 v` if `v ‚ąą W` and `.unsplit v` otherwise, and `toCopy1 W` is the symmetric `.copy1` / `.unsplit` helper. So the directed-edge field `G.E.image (fun e => (toCopy1 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2))` reads transparently as the LN's $\{(v_1^i, v_2^o) \mid (v_1, v_2) \in E\}$.

The definition uses Lean's `where` syntax, naming each of the four data fields (`J`, `V`, `E`, `L`) alongside the five CDMG proof obligations (`hJV_disj`, `hE_subset`, `hL_subset`, `hL_irrefl`, `hL_symm`). The five obligations are discharged by private lemmas declared above the def, so the def body itself shows just the four data assignments plus references to the named proof lemmas ‚ÄĒ no proof-tactic clutter at the point of definition. Neither `hG` nor `hW` is consumed in the body: a file-scoped `set_option linter.unusedVariables false in` directive suppresses the warning. `hG`'s acyclicity is only used downstream by `claim_3_9`, which proves the SWIG itself is acyclic.
design_choices:
**The tagged node universe reuses `def_3_11`'s `SplitNode Node` rather than introducing a fresh `SwigNode`.** The LN realises $W^o$ and $W^i$ as type-level tagged copies so the five disjointness assertions hold by typing. `SplitNode Node`, introduced for the node-splitting graph in `def_3_11`, is already exactly such a tagged-sum carrier ‚ÄĒ three constructors `unsplit`, `copy0`, `copy1` with decidable equality ‚ÄĒ and admits the SWIG-side reading `.copy0 ‚ÜĒ ^o`, `.copy1 ‚ÜĒ ^i` directly. Reusing it has three payoffs: the SWIG and the node-split graph live on the *same* carrier (so any future lemma relating them is a literal equation, not a coercion threaded through every claim); the `toCopy0` and `toCopy1` shorthand helpers are already defined; and the constructor semantics align ‚ÄĒ `.copy0` was already the 'natural / observational' side in `def_3_11`, matching SWIG's $^o$. A parallel `inductive SwigNode` would have duplicated structural lemmas and forced a `SwigNode ‚ÜĒ SplitNode` coercion at every downstream composition site.

**The construction is direct (four set-builders) rather than the composition `(G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn (W.image .copy1) _`.** The SWIG admits a conceptual reading as 'node-split at $W$, then hard-intervene at $W^i$', and the composed Lean form is type-correct. Three reasons to write the set-builders directly. The direct form matches the LN's item-by-item statement, so the website view shows four clauses line by line rather than a composite of two unrelated operators. Definitional unfolding for downstream membership queries reduces to one `Finset.mem_image` per field, rather than a two-stage `mem_filter`-of-`mem_image`-of-`mem_union` chain. And the composed form would introduce transfer edges `(.copy0 w, .copy1 w)` that the outer hard intervention immediately removes; the direct form simply never includes them, realising the LN's 'nothing is included and then removed' framing literally.

**Acyclicity is handled asymmetrically: `hG : G.IsCADMG` sits on the input signature, but the output type is `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`, not a CADMG.** The LN's 'Let $G$ be a CADMG' is a hypothesis on the input domain, so the binder belongs on the signature; dropping it would silently widen the LN's input quantifier to admit non-acyclic graphs. The four field assignments and the five CDMG obligations genuinely do not consume `hG`, exactly as the LN spec's 'paradigm observation' paragraph asserts. Acyclicity *preservation* ‚ÄĒ that the SWIG of an acyclic graph is itself acyclic ‚ÄĒ is deferred to `claim_3_9`, mirroring the chapter-3 pattern: `def_3_10` (`hardInterventionOn`) returns `CDMG` and lifts acyclicity in `claim_3_3`; `def_3_11` (`nodeSplittingOn`) returns `CDMG` and lifts the topological-order property in `claim_3_6`.

**The LN's closing 'removing all edges into $W^i$ / out of $W^o$' is realised structurally, not as a deletion step.** Item iii.'s set-builder `(toCopy1 W e.1, toCopy0 W e.2)` can never produce a `.copy1`-tagged target nor a `.copy0`-tagged source; item iv.'s symmetric `toCopy0`-on-both-endpoints construction cannot produce a `.copy1` endpoint at either slot. So no directed edge runs into $W^i$ or out of $W^o$, and no bidirected edge touches $W^i$ at all ‚ÄĒ not because such edges are filtered out, but because they are never included to begin with. The LN's procedurally-phrased 'removal' translates to its structural referent.
